overnight_news: **Iran expands strikes across Middle East**, escalating tensions and threatening Strait of Hormuz; oil spikes (Brent $81, WTI $74), pressuring inflation and Fed cuts. Affects: Oil majors (XOM, CVX), DJI, SPX, COMP.[1][2]
**Trump orders US financial guarantees and potential Navy escorts for Gulf oil tankers**, easing oil prices slightly after initial surge; aims to ensure energy flow amid threats.[1]
**Tehran threatens attacks on vessels in Strait of Hormuz**, a key chokepoint for 20% of world oil; drives up shipping rates and energy prices.[1]
**US stocks drop sharply (Dow -403 pts or 0.83%, S&P -0.94%, Nasdaq -1.02%)** on March 3 amid Middle East fears and oil-driven inflation worries.[1][2]
**Experts warn oil markets overly optimistic on Hormuz resumption; risk of $100/bbl if disruptions persist**, per Rapidan Energy Group (March 4).[4]
